engineIntelligence.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the engines fitted to the Rolls Royce Ghost in the UK - including known failure causes, production years across variants, typical replacement costs, and key technical specs. All prices reflect typical UK market ranges for rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost N74B66 engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented failure between 60k-100k miles on N74B66 units.
Turbo wastegate sticking - actuator wear causes boost loss and rattling noises.
High-pressure fuel pump degradation - reduced pressure leads to rough running and misfires.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost N74B68 engine - petrol 6.75L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector coking on direct injection - carbon buildup affects fuel delivery at higher mileage.
Turbo oil seal wear - minor leaks develop on turbocharger housings after extended use.
ECU calibration retention required - specialist coding needed post-replacement to avoid limp mode.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £12,400-£28,900 (supply only) Estimated from comparable N74B68 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Rolls Royce Ghost N74B66TU engine - petrol 6.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Performance calibration sensitivity - ECU mapping must be retained to preserve Black Badge output.
Wastegate actuator wear under load - high-output tuning accelerates turbo component fatigue.
Valvetronic motor strain - variable valve lift system requires specialist calibration post-rebuild.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £14,200-£32,500 (supply only) Specialist performance rebuild - pricing reflects calibration retention and dyno validation.

commonProblems.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Rolls Royce Ghost's N74 V12 family is refined but not immune to wear; timing chain tensioners, turbo wastegates and high-pressure fuel pumps dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide between repair and replacement.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why N74B66 Engines Fail Between 60k-100k Miles
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost SWB, EWB, Series II, Black Badge (2009-2020, N74B66 & N74B66TU)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise and potential valve timing drift.

repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioners, guides, seals; full front-end teardown
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause; recommended if noise present or mileage >80k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; eliminates all timing-related risks

problems.recommendation: On a Ghost valued £80k-£150k, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles is cost-effective. If rattling is present or mileage exceeds 90k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt N74B66 engine prices for your Rolls Royce Ghost

problems.h4: Turbo Wastegate Sticking - Boost Loss and Rattling on N74 Ghost Engines
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost all variants (2009-present, N74B66, N74B66TU, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: Wastegate actuator linkage wear or carbon buildup causes incomplete closure, leading to boost leaks and rattling under deceleration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace or recalibrate wastegate actuator; turbo removal not always required
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if turbo internals are sound; quick fix for early-stage symptoms

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace wastegate mechanism, seals, bearings; bench-test
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses internal wear; recommended if boost loss or oil leaks present

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74 Engine with Turbo Refresh
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with refurbished or new turbos, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if multiple turbo issues or high mileage; eliminates repeat failures

problems.recommendation: If rattling is intermittent and boost is stable, actuator replacement may suffice. For persistent boost loss or oil consumption, a turbo rebuild or full engine replacement with refreshed turbos offers greater peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for N74 turbo service or rebuilt Ghost engine

problems.h4: High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Rough Running and Misfires on N74 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost all variants (2009-present, N74B66, N74B66TU, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: HPFP internal wear reduces fuel pressure, causing lean conditions, misfires, and potential catalytic converter damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace high-pressure fuel pump; fuel system flush; ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if pump is sole failure point; requires genuine or OEM-spec part

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, injectors, lines; full system clean and pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if injectors show wear or contamination; prevents repeat failures

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74 with Upgraded Fuel System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded HPFP and injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear or high mileage; eliminates fuel-related risks

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure is often isolated; replacement with an OEM-spec unit is usually sufficient. If misfires persist or injectors are contaminated, a fuel system overhaul or rebuilt engine offers more comprehensive protection.
problems.cta: Compare HPFP replacement vs rebuilt Ghost engine costs

problems.group: Injector Coking on N74B68 Direct Injection
problems.h4: Injector Coking on N74B68 Direct Injection - Carbon Buildup and Misfires
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost RR2 variants (2020-present, N74B68)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design leads to carbon accumulation on injector tips, affecting spray pattern and combustion efficiency.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, ultrasonic clean, flow test, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early carbon buildup; non-invasive and cost-effective

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors with OEM-spec units; ECU adaptation and coding
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if cleaning fails or injectors show physical wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt N74B68 with Refreshed Fuel System
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new injectors, HPFP, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has other wear or high mileage; eliminates fuel system risks

problems.recommendation: Start with ultrasonic cleaning if symptoms are mild. For persistent misfires or rough idle, injector replacement or a rebuilt engine with refreshed fuel components offers longer-term reliability.
problems.cta: Enquire about N74B68 injector service or rebuilt Ghost engine

problems.h4: ECU Calibration Loss Post-Replacement - Limp Mode and Performance Loss
problems.affectedModels: Rolls Royce Ghost all variants (2009-present, all N74 codes)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: N/A (occurs post-service if calibration not retained)
problems.rootCause: Engine replacement or major service without ISTA+/Rheingold coding causes ECU to default to limp mode, losing Valvetronic, turbo mapping, and performance calibration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Connect to ISTA+, code new engine serial, adapt Valvetronic, reset adaptations
repairOptions.longevity: Essential after any engine swap; prevents limp mode and restores performance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: ECU coding plus bench dyno test to verify torque curve, boost mapping, smoothness
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for Black Badge or performance variants; ensures calibration integrity

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Pre-Coded ECU
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with ECU pre-coded to vehicle, dyno-validated, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for hassle-free installation; eliminates post-fit coding risks

problems.recommendation: ECU calibration is non-negotiable after engine replacement. Always confirm your specialist includes ISTA+ coding and Valvetronic adaptation. For Black Badge, insist on dyno validation to preserve performance mapping.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ghost engine replacement with ECU calibration included

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Rolls Royce Ghost
finalCta.paragraph: The N74 V12 is a masterpiece of refinement, but known failure points like timing chain tensioners, turbo wastegates and fuel pumps can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Rolls Royce Ghost specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these original weaknesses. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Rolls Royce Ghost Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Rolls Royce Ghost and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

High replacement demand driven by ultra-luxury ownership expectations and warranty coverage priorities

engineYears.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Rolls Royce Ghost production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Ghost launched with the N74B66 6.6L V12, establishing the benchmark for ultra-luxury refinement.
keyChanges: Rolls Royce Ghost debut with all-new RR1 platform
N74B66 6.6L twin-turbo V12 introduced across SWB and EWB variants
knownFor: Exceptional refinement and effortless torque delivery
Early examples now entering higher-mileage replacement territory
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges as primary failure point
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009 Rolls Royce Ghost replacement engines
years.ticker: Rolls Royce Ghost N74B66 engine replacement • Ghost timing chain tensioner fix • Ghost V12 rebuilt engine cost
enquiries: Rolls Royce Ghost N74B66 engine replacement

years.description: The 2014 facelift refreshed styling and technology while retaining the proven N74B66 powertrain.
keyChanges: Series II facelift with updated LED lighting and interior technology
N74B66 carried over with minor calibration updates
Black Badge variant introduced in 2016 with N74B66TU technical update
knownFor: Improved infotainment and driver assistance features
Black Badge models command premium replacement pricing due to performance calibration
N74B66TU requires specialist ECU coding to retain output and mapping
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014 Rolls Royce Ghost replacement engines
years.ticker: Ghost Black Badge engine replacement • N74B66TU calibration retention • Series II V12 rebuild cost

years.preview: Present - Second Generation Architecture of Luxury
years.description: The all-new RR2 platform introduced the updated N74B68 6.75L V12 with refined emissions compliance and enhanced refinement.
keyChanges: All-new Architecture of Luxury platform (RR2) launched
N74B68 6.75L V12 introduced with updated emissions hardware
Black Badge RR2 variants feature further calibration enhancements
knownFor: Enhanced refinement and reduced NVH over first generation
Direct injection design introduces injector coking as new failure consideration
Replacement pricing reflects newer technology and lower UK availability to date
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020 Rolls Royce Ghost replacement engines
years.ticker: Ghost RR2 engine replacement • N74B68 injector coking fix • New Ghost V12 rebuilt cost

faq.h2: Rolls Royce Ghost Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Rolls Royce Ghost engines typically range from £9,850 to £28,900 supply-only, depending on engine code, variant and condition. Black Badge and second-generation models command premium pricing due to calibration complexity and lower availability.
keyPoints: N74B66 rebuilt units start around £9,850 for standard variants
N74B68 and Black Badge N74B66TU range £12,400-£32,500
Supply & fit adds £2,800-£6,500 depending on specialist location
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Rolls Royce Ghost engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine code is in my Rolls Royce Ghost?
items.answer: First-generation Ghost (2009-2020) uses N74B66 or N74B66TU (Black Badge); second-generation (2020-present) uses N74B68. Enter your registration above to instantly verify your exact engine code via DVLA.
keyPoints: N74B66TU: Black Badge technical update, 593hp, calibration-critical
N74B68: 6.75L V12, 563-593hp, 2020-present RR2 platform
VIN decoder and DVLA lookup available via our registration tool
items.cta: Verify your Rolls Royce Ghost engine code instantly

items.question: How long does a Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 3-7 working days from order confirmation. Full supply & fit installation requires 5-10 working days depending on specialist workload and ECU calibration requirements.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines are bench-tested before dispatch for quality assurance
Specialist fitting centres require booking in advance due to complexity
ECU coding and Valvetronic adaptation add 1-2 days to installation timeline
Nationwide delivery available with insured pallet shipping
items.cta: Get a timeline estimate for your Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacement

items.question: Is a rebuilt Rolls Royce Ghost engine as good as new?
items.answer: Professionally rebuilt N74 engines include upgraded timing components, refreshed turbos and dyno validation, often exceeding original reliability when installed by a specialist with proper ECU calibration.
keyPoints: Rebuilt units replace wear items like tensioners, guides and seals
Upgraded components address known failure points from original design
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind
Specialist installation with ISTA+ coding ensures performance retention
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used Rolls Royce Ghost engine options

items.question: What warranty comes with a Rolls Royce Ghost replacement engine?
items.answer: Every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour. Some specialists offer extended 24-month coverage for remanufactured units.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months, unlimited mileage, UK-wide coverage
Remanufactured units may include 24-month options from select suppliers
Warranty covers internal components, turbos and fuel system parts
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: View warranty terms for Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacements

items.question: Can I fit a Rolls Royce Ghost engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible, Ghost engine replacement requires specialist tools, ISTA+ coding equipment and Rolls Royce-specific expertise. We strongly recommend using a vetted specialist from our network.
keyPoints: N74 V12 removal requires engine crane, transmission support and specialist tools
ECU calibration with ISTA+ is essential to avoid limp mode and performance loss
Valvetronic adaptation and turbo mapping require Rolls Royce diagnostic access
Incorrect installation voids warranty and risks catastrophic engine damage
items.cta: Find a vetted Rolls Royce Ghost engine fitting specialist near you

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Rolls Royce Ghost warranty?
items.answer: If your Ghost is still under manufacturer warranty, using a non-Rolls Royce replacement engine may affect coverage. However, EngineMarket suppliers offer 12-month warranties that provide equivalent protection for out-of-warranty vehicles.
keyPoints: Manufacturer warranty typically requires Rolls Royce-approved parts and fitting
Independent rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty suit out-of-warranty vehicles
Always disclose replacement history when selling to maintain transparency
Specialist documentation and dyno reports support future resale value
items.cta: Compare warranty options for your Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacement

items.question: How do I know if my Rolls Royce Ghost needs an engine replacement?
items.answer: Key indicators include persistent rattling (timing chain), boost loss or turbo rattling, rough running with misfires, excessive oil consumption, or ECU limp mode that persists after basic diagnostics.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattling between 60k-100k miles suggests tensioner wear
Turbo wastegate sticking causes boost loss and deceleration rattling
Misfires with fuel pressure codes may indicate HPFP or injector failure
Professional compression and leak-down testing confirms internal wear
items.cta: Get a diagnostic quote for your Rolls Royce Ghost engine concerns

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned, and remanufactured Ghost engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are cleaned and resealed; rebuilt engines replace wear items and address known failures; remanufactured units are zero-hour builds with new internals to factory tolerances.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: inspection, cleaning, gasket/seal renewal, basic testing
Rebuilt: replacement of tensioners, guides, seals, turbo refresh, dyno validation
Remanufactured: new pistons, bearings, machining to factory specs, performance calibration
Pricing reflects scope: reconditioned £9,850+, rebuilt £12,400+, remanufactured £18,500+
items.cta: Compare rebuild types for your Rolls Royce Ghost engine replacement

items.question: Do you offer supply and fit for Rolls Royce Ghost engines?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists offering full supply & fit service including engine delivery, specialist installation, ECU coding, Valvetronic adaptation and post-install validation.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £2,800-£6,500 to engine price depending on variant and location
Service includes ISTA+ coding, limp mode prevention and road test validation
Black Badge and performance variants require calibration retention expertise
Nationwide network covers all UK regions with insured delivery and fitting
items.cta: Find Rolls Royce Ghost engine supply & fit deals near you
